name: Notify the team on new events with Discord at scale

parameters.content: =✅ Notify the team on new events with Discord at scale — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf05997-job`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Route by Work Type

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: standard, priority + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'standard' → Google Sheets · ②→ Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert · ③→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'standard' → Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Job Tracker', matched on `jobId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Next: Merge Routed Work

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

Next: Merge Routed Work

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

Appends a new row to 'Job Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Next: Merge Routed Work

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Send Summary to Discord

### Send Summary to Discord

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

# Notify the team on new events with Discord at scale

**Pattern:** Event Notification · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Notify the team on new events with Discord at scale. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual process orchestration is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Route by Work Type → Process 'standard' → Google Sheets → Merge Routed Work → Align Field Names → Send Summary to Discord

## Use it for
- Teams that want to event notification without manual effort
- Anyone automating process orchestration in productivity
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Unrecognized work types are parked in a dead-letter lane instead of erroring the whole run or silently dropping the item — new event kinds will show up eventually, and this is where you'd notice one without losing it. One kind is deliberately routed through a sub-workflow to show how heavier per-type logic can be extracted without touching this router at all.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Send a work-type value that matches no switch rule and confirm it lands in the dead-letter branch.
2. Add a new work type and its own handling branch.
3. Replace the direct-write branch with a sub-workflow call, matching the pattern already used for the first kind.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your process orchestration process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
